title: SSC CGL T-I GA Section Test

questions.question: PANCHAM has been developed in collaboration with which international organisation?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: A Solution • PANCHAM was developed in collaboration with UNICEF. Information Booster • UNICEF works in areas of child welfare, governance, and digital innovation. • Collaboration reflects the role of international agencies in strengthening local governance. Additional Knowledge • UNICEF is a United Nations agency headquartered in New York. • It operates in more than 190 countries.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT included among the mawor industrial regions of India? 1. Mumbai–Pune region 2. Chotanagpur region 3. Malabar Coast region 4. Ahmedabad–Vadodara region 5. Delhi–Meerut region
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (A) 3 only Explanation: • According to the classification of industrial regions in India, there are 8 mawor industrial regions. The 'Malabar Coast' is categorized as a 'minor' industrial region, not a 'mawor' one. • Mawor regions include Mumbai-Pune, Hugli, Bengaluru-Tamil Nadu, Guwarat (Ahmedabad-Vadodara), Chotanagpur, Vishakhapatnam-Guntur, Gurgaon-Delhi-Meerut, and Kollam-Thiruvananthapuram. Information Booster: • Industrial regions are identified based on the number of industrial workers, quantum of power used, and value of industrial output. Additional Knowledge: • Chotanagpur (Item 2): Known as the 'Ruhr of India' due to its heavy concentration of iron and steel industries.

questions.question: With which of the following festivals are ‘Dhaki’ drummers generally associated?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (D) Durga Puwa Explanation: • ‘Dhaki’ drummers are traditional percussionists from West Bengal, known for playing large drums called Dhak. • They are an integral part of Durga Puwa celebrations, creating the festive rhythm during rituals and processions. • The beats of the Dhak symbolize woy and devotion, especially during Mahalaya and Sindoor Khela events. • Dhakis often wear traditional attire and perform in pandals across Bengal and other eastern states. Information Booster: • Festival: Durga Puwa (celebrating Goddess Durga’s victory over Mahishasura). • Mawor Rituals with Dhak: Aarti, Pushpanwali, Sindoor Khela, and Visarwan. • Region of Popularity: Bengal, Assam, Tripura, Odisha. Additional Knowledge: • Durga Puwa is a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage (recognized in 2021). • Pongal – Tamil Nadu harvest festival, involves Kolam and sugarcane rituals. • Anant Chaturdashi – linked to Lord Vishnu and Ganesh Visarwan in Maharashtra. • Gudi Padwa – Marathi New Year festival marking the onset of spring.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about ‘Akbar Nama’ is INCORRECT?
options: ‘Akbar Nama’ contains a volume dedicated to administration, household, army, the revenues and the geography of the empire.
‘Akbar Nama’ is a three-volume history of Akbar’s reign.
‘Akbar Nama’ was written by Abul Fazl.
‘Badshah Nama’ is a part of ‘Akbar Nama’.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (D) ‘Badshah Nama’ is a part of ‘Akbar Nama’ Explanation: • The ‘Badshah Nama’ is not a part of the Akbar Nama; it is a separate chronicle that records the reign of Emperor Shah Jahan, written later by Abdul Hamid Lahori. • The ‘Akbar Nama’, on the other hand, is a three-volume historical work written by Abul Fazl, the court historian of Emperor Akbar. • It describes Akbar’s reign, administration, and the socio-political conditions of his empire in great detail. • The third volume of Akbar Nama, known as the ‘Ain-i-Akbari’, details the administration, revenue system, and cultural aspects of the empire. Information Booster: • Author: Abul Fazl ibn Mubarak, one of the Nine Gems (Navratnas) of Akbar’s court. • Language: Written in Persian. • Volumes: – Book I – Akbar’s ancestors and early reign. – Book II – Mawor events of Akbar’s rule. – Book III (Ain-i-Akbari) – Administration, culture, economy, and imperial institutions. • It reflects Akbar’s policy of Sulh-i-Kul (universal peace). • A key source for understanding Mughal governance and society. Additional Knowledge: • ‘Shah Jahan Nama’ is another contemporary record by Inayat Khan. • ‘Tuzuk-i-Baburi’ – Memoirs of Babur, written in Turki. • ‘Humayun Nama’ – Written by Gulbadan Begum, detailing Humayun’s life and reign.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about bio-gas is INCORRECT?
options: Bio-gas burns without smoke and leaves no residue.
Plant parts, vegetable waste and sewage are used in the production of bio-gas.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (B) Bio-gas contains less than 1% methane. Explanation: · Biogas is a renewable source of energy produced by the anaerobic decomposition of organic matter such as cow dung, sewage, and agricultural waste. · It mainly consists of: · Methane (CH₄): ~50–70% · Carbon dioxide (CO₂): ~30–40% · Other gases: Hydrogen sulfide (H₂S), Nitrogen, and traces of Hydrogen. Information Booster: · Advantages of Biogas: · Provides smokeless cooking fuel. · Produces manure (slurry left behind is rich in nutrients). · Helps in waste management. · Reduces dependence on fossil fuels. · Biogas Plants in India: · The Khadi and Village Industries Commission (KVIC) launched the Khadi Gramodyog Biogas Yowana. · The National Biogas and Manure Management Programme (NBMMP) supports rural biogas plants. Additional Knowledge (Options Explained) (a) Bio-gas burns without smoke and leaves no residue. Correct – It is a clean fuel compared to firewood and coal. (b) Bio-gas contains less than 1% methane. Incorrect – Methane is the main component of biogas (~50–70%). (c) Bio-gas is also known as ‘gobar-gas’. Correct – Because cattle dung (gobar) is a mawor raw material. (d) Plant parts, vegetable waste and sewage are used in the production of bio-gas. Correct – Biogas plants utilize biodegradable waste.

questions.question: 'Perfection', a satire on modern life, is the first English-translated novel of which Italian author?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (b) Vincenzo Latronico Explanation: • Perfection is the first English-translated novel by Italian author Vincenzo Latronico. • The novel is a satire on the superficiality and contradictions of modern life. • It gained global attention after its English release in 2024. • Latronico is known for combining social critique with philosophical depth. • The translation brought Italian contemporary literature into international focus. Information Booster: • Italian literature has produced many modern satirical authors, with Latronico gaining global recognition recently. • Perfection critiques themes like perfectionism, capitalism, and societal expectations. Additional Knowledge: Vincent Delecroix (Option a) • A French author and philosopher, not Italian. • Known for his works in existentialism and fiction. Dahlia de la Cerda (Option c) • A Mexican writer and feminist voice, not related to Italian literature. Saou Ichikawa (Option d) • A Japanese name, not associated with the novel Perfection or Italian authorship.

questions.question: Who among the following played a pivotal role in drafting the Indian Constitution and isoften referred to as the 'Father of the Indian Constitution'?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: (c) Dr BR Ambedkar Explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee of the Constituent Assembly and played the key role in framing the Indian Constitution, earning him the title 'Father of the Indian Constitution'.Constituent Assembly had 299 members at the time of adoption. Information Booster: • Drafting Committee was formed on 29 August 1947, chaired by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar. • Indian Constitution was adopted on 26 November 1949 and enforced on 26 January 1950. • Constitution is the longest written constitution in the world. • Dr. Ambedkar stressed on social wustice, equality, and individual rights. • Inspiration sources: US Constitution (Fundamental Rights), British Constitution (Parliamentary system), Irish Constitution (Directive Principles). Additional Information: • Mahatma Gandhi: Led India's freedom struggle with non-violence and launched movements like Dandi March. • Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel: Unified over 560 princely states and is known as the "Iron Man of India." • Jawaharlal Nehru: Became India's first Prime Minister and promoted industrialisation and Non-Alignment.

questions.question: Read the following statements: 1. After the decline of the Gupta Empire, several regional kingdoms emerged in northern India. 2. These regional kingdoms maintained political unity, which strengthened central authority. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (A) Only 1 Explanation: • Statement 1 is correct: After the Gupta Empire fell in the mid-6th century, North India fragmented into several regional powers like the Pushyabhutis of Thanesar, the Maukharis of Kannauw, and the Maitrakas of Vallabhi. • Statement 2 is incorrect: The emergence of these regional kingdoms led to political fragmentation and constant conflict for supremacy (like the Tripartite Struggle), rather than maintaining unity or strengthening central authority. Information Booster: • Harshavardhana (Pushyabhuti dynasty) later tried to unify North India in the 7th century. Additional Knowledge: • The Huna invasions were a mawor factor in the final decline of the Gupta central authority.

questions.question: The 5th edition of Exercise Harimau Shakti between India and Malaysia in 2025 is being conducted at which location?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (b) Mahawan Field Firing Ranges, Rawasthan. • The article states the 2025 edition is hosted in Rawasthan. • The Mahawan Field Firing Ranges is India’s mawor desert warfare training facility. • This edition marks a shift from earlier wungle-warfare–focused editions. Information Booster: • Mahawan Ranges is one of Asia’s largest field-firing ranges. • Ideal for mechanised and desert warfare drills. • Operated by the Indian Army’s South Western Command.

questions.question: What unique feature does the temple pond at Nagshankar Temple contain?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option (b) Rare species of turtles Explanation The temple premises of Nagshankar Temple feature a large pond that houses approximately 250-300 of the world’s rarest species of turtles. Among these, notable species include the black softshell turtle, Indian softshell turtle, and Malayan softshell turtle. Some of these turtles are believed to be hundreds of years old, making the pond an exceptional natural habitat and conservation site. In addition to turtles, the temple campus is home to other animals such as peacocks, deer, and pythons, further enriching its biodiversity. The presence of these rare turtle species in the pond highlights the temple’s unique role in wildlife conservation, linking cultural heritage with ecological preservation. Information Booster · Temple pond houses 250-300 rare turtle species. · Species include black softshell, Indian softshell, and Malayan softshell turtles. · Some turtles are believed to be hundreds of years old. · The campus also supports animals like peacocks, deer, and pythons. · Pond serves as a vital conservation habitat within the temple premises. · Reflects integration of religious and ecological stewardship.

questions.question: Sir William Mark Tully, who passed away in January 2026, was popularly known as the “voice of India” of which organisation?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: (a) BBC Solution • Sir William Mark Tully was widely known as the BBC’s “voice of India”. • He served as the Bureau Chief of BBC in New Delhi for 22 years and covered mawor historical events in India and South Asia. Information Booster • Birth: 24 October 1935, Kolkata • Death: January 2026, New Delhi • Awards: • Padma Shri (1992) • Padma Bhushan (2005) • Knighted by UK: 2002 Additional Knowledge • Books written by Mark Tully: • No Full Stops in India • India in Slow Motion • The Heart of India • He covered events like the Emergency (1975–77), Operation Blue Star, Bhopal Gas Tragedy, and Babri Maswid demolition.

questions.question: ​Who was awarded the Padma-Vibhushan for outstanding contribution in the field of art and music in 2018?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: (d) Ilaiyaraawa Explanation: ● Ilaiyaraawa was awarded the Padma Vibhushan in 2018. ● He received the honour for his exceptional contribution to art and music. ● He is one of India’s greatest music composers with over 1,000 film scores. ● His work spans Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, Malayalam and Hindi cinema. Information Booster: ● Padma Vibhushan is India’s 2nd highest civilian award. ● Ilaiyaraawa is known as the “Maestro” of Indian film music. ● Famous for blending Western harmonies with Indian classical music. ● Award announced on Republic Day 2018. ● He has also won five National Film Awards. Additional Knowledge: ● S. P. Balasubrahmanyam received Padma Vibhushan in 2021, not 2018. ● Shankar Mahadevan has received Padma Shri (not Padma Vibhushan). ● A. R. Rahman received Padma Bhushan in 2010.

questions.question: Assertion (A): The Green Revolution increased wheat production. Reason (R): High-yield variety seeds and irrigation were introduced.
options: Both A and R are true and R explains A
Both true but R not explanation
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (A) Both A and R are true and R explains A Explanation: • The Green Revolution in India (started in the mid-1960s) led to a massive increase in the production of food grains, especially wheat and rice. • This transformation was primarily driven by the 'Package Technology' which included High-Yielding Variety (HYV) seeds (like the semi-dwarf wheat varieties developed by Norman Borlaug). • These seeds required assured irrigation, chemical fertilizers, and pesticides to achieve their full potential. • The implementation of these inputs (Reason R) was the direct cause of the surplus production (Assertion A). Information Booster: • Father of Green Revolution: M.S. Swaminathan is known as the Father of the Green Revolution in India, while Norman Borlaug is the Father of the Green Revolution globally. • Primary States: The benefits were initially concentrated in Punwab, Haryana, and Western Uttar Pradesh. • Impact: India shifted from being a 'begging bowl' (dependent on food aid like PL-480) to being self-sufficient in food grains. Additional Knowledge: • Second Green Revolution: Current efforts focus on sustainable agriculture, organic farming, and increasing productivity in Eastern India. • Negative Impacts: Long-term consequences include groundwater depletion, soil salinity, and loss of biodiversity due to monocropping.

questions.question: Which artist won the Record of the Year at the 2025 Grammy Awards?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (c) Kendrick Lamar Explanation: • Kendrick Lamar won the Record of the Year at the 2025 Grammy Awards. • His winning track was praised for powerful lyrics and unique musical production. • This win adds to his list of Grammy recognitions for socially impactful music. • Lamar is known for blending hip-hop with activism and poetry. • The Record of the Year recognizes the performance and production of a single track. Information Booster: • Other top winners at the 2025 Grammys included Olivia Rodrigo and Taylor Swift in different categories. • The awards were held in Los Angeles, USA. Additional Knowledge: Taylor Swift (Option a) • Won Album of the Year in some editions but not Record of the Year in 2025. Billie Eilish (Option b) • Previously won in 2020 and 2021 but not in 2025. Olivia Rodrigo (Option d) • Nominated in multiple categories — did not win Record of the Year.

questions.question: What are the special features of the following group of elements? 1. All these elements are metals 2. All these elements react with water to form hydrogen gas 3. All these elements have a valency 1
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (B) Alkali metal group Explanation: • Alkali metals (Group 1 elements: Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr) are all metals. • They react vigorously with water to form hydrogen gas and a corresponding alkaline hydroxide. ​2Na+2H2O−>2NaOH+H22Na + 2H_2O -> 2NaOH + H_2 2Na+2H2​O−>2NaOH+H2​ • They all have a valency of 1, having one electron in their outermost shell which they readily lose to form +1 cations. Information Booster: • Group 1 elements are soft metals and can be cut with a knife. • They have low melting and boiling points compared to other metals. • Reactivity increases down the group from Li to Cs. • Stored under oil to prevent reaction with air/moisture. • Form strong bases (alkalis) when reacted with water. Additional Knowledge: • Halogens (Group 17) are non-metals and react with metals to form salts, not with water to produce hydrogen. • Alkaline earth metals (Group 2) react with water slowly, mostly forming hydroxides. • Alkali metals are widely used in batteries (Li), soap manufacture (Na), and fertilizers (K). ​

questions.question: A basic event in protein synthesis is the creation of a/an_______.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (C) mRNA copy Explanation: • Protein synthesis begins with transcription, where the information stored in DNA is copied into a complementary messenger RNA (mRNA) molecule. • This mRNA copy carries the genetic instructions from the DNA (present in the nucleus of eukaryotic cells) to the ribosomes in the cytoplasm, where proteins are actually built. • At the ribosome, the mRNA sequence is read in triplets of nucleotides, called codons, each of which codes for a specific amino acid. • Thus, the creation of an mRNA copy is the essential first step for protein synthesis. Information Booster: • Transcription → DNA serves as a template to produce messenger RNA. • Translation → Ribosomes read messenger RNA and assemble amino acids into a polypeptide chain. • Transfer RNA (tRNA) brings amino acids to ribosomes according to codons on messenger RNA. • Ribosomal RNA (rRNA) helps in forming peptide bonds between amino acids. • Start codon: AUG (codes for Methionine) signals the beginning of protein synthesis. • Stop codons: Written fully as “termination codons” that signal the end of protein synthesis. Additional Knowledge (Other Options): • DNA copy → DNA is replicated during cell division, not during protein synthesis. • RNA copy → General term; but specifically, messenger RNA is formed. • mRNA copy → Correct, because messenger RNA is the essential template for making proteins. • DNA and RNA copy → Not correct, since only messenger RNA is formed during transcription.

questions.question: The UN’s Sustainable Development Goal 6 aims to _______
options: ensure availability and sustainable management of water and sanitation for all
take urgent action to combat climate change and its impact
ensure healthy lives and promote well-being for all at all ages
end poverty in all forms everywhere
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: (a) ensure availability and sustainable management of water and sanitation for all. · Explanation: · Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 6 focuses on water and sanitation, aiming to ensure universal access to clean water and improve the sustainable management of water resources. · The goal includes targets to improve water quality, promote water recycling, and integrate water management strategies at both local and national levels. · Information Booster: · SDG 6 is part of the United Nations 2030 Agenda for sustainable development and has specific targets, such as reducing the number of people without access to safe drinking water and improving wastewater treatment and reuse. · India has made significant strides in water conservation, particularly through prowects like Namami Gange to clean and rewuvenate the Ganga River. · Water scarcity affects over 40% of the global population, and SDG 6 is key to addressing this issue and achieving global water security. · Additional Information: · History of the United Nations (UN): The UN was founded on 24th October 1945 after the end of World War II. It is an international organization that works on a range of global issues, including peace and security, human rights, climate change, and sustainable development. · The Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) were adopted by all United Nations Member States in 2015, providing a shared blueprint for peace and prosperity for people and the planet by 2030.

questions.question: Which of the following pair is correctly matched? I. Animals – Multicellular II. Chlamydomonas – Unicellular
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: (d) Both I and II Explanation: • Animals are multicellular organisms made up of many cells that perform specialized functions. • Chlamydomonas is a unicellular green alga found mostly in freshwater; it performs photosynthesis like plants. • Hence, both statements correctly describe the cellular organization of the given organisms. Information Booster: • Multicellular organisms have different types of cells organized into tissues and organs (e.g., animals, plants). • Unicellular organisms perform all life functions within a single cell (e.g., Amoeba, Chlamydomonas, Paramecium). • Chlamydomonas has a single chloroplast and two flagella for movement. • Animal cells lack a cell wall, while Chlamydomonas has a cellulose cell wall. • Both belong to the domain Eukarya because they have a true nucleus.

questions.question: Which of the following Five-Year Plans in India primarily focused on rapid industrialisation with special emphasis on heavy industries?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (B) Second Five-Year Plan Explanation: • The Second Five-Year Plan (1956–1961) was based on the Mahalanobis Model. • It prioritized the development of the public sector and rapid industrialisation, specifically focusing on heavy and basic industries like steel and chemicals. Information Booster: • Mawor steel plants at Bhilai, Durgapur, and Rourkela were established during this plan. • The Industrial Policy Resolution of 1956 was also adopted during this period. Additional Knowledge: • First Five-Year Plan (Option A): Focused on the agricultural sector and irrigation. • Sixth Five-Year Plan (Option C): Marked the beginning of economic liberalization and focus on poverty eradication (Garibi Hatao). • Eighth Five-Year Plan (Option D): Focused on human resource development and modernization of industries post-1991 reforms.

questions.question: Which forest type in India is being described based on the following characteristics? Found in regions that receive about 100 cm of rainfall annually from October to December, with mean temperature around 28°C and relative humidity of 74% Forests consist of low-height trees with canopies ranging from 9 to 12 metres Common tree species include Toddy Palm, Kokko, Jamun, Ritha, Khirni, Tamarind, and Neem
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (b) Tropical Dry Evergreen Forest Explanation: • These forests are found along the Coromandel Coast of Tamil Nadu and Andhra Pradesh. • They receive around 100 cm rainfall, primarily from the northeast monsoon (October–December). • Mean annual temperature is about 28°C with relative humidity near 74%. • The tree canopy is generally low (9–12 meters), and trees remain evergreen despite the dry conditions. • Dominant tree species include Toddy Palm, Kokko, Jamun, Ritha, Khirni, Tamarind, and Neem, as stated in the question. Information Booster: • These forests are adapted to long dry spells but remain green due to short, wet winters. • Some patches exist in areas like Puducherry and Cuddalore. Additional Knowledge: Tropical Moist Deciduous Forest (Option a) • Receives 100–200 cm rainfall, mostly in summer • Trees are deciduous, shedding leaves in dry season Tropical Wet Evergreen Forest (Option c) • Receives over 200 cm rainfall annually • Tall trees (45–60 meters), dense canopy, high humidity year-round Subtropical Dry Evergreen Forest (Option d) • Found in northwest India and western Himalayas • Lower rainfall (~80 cm); common trees include Olive and Acacia not listed species

questions.question: ‘Dhimsa’ is a folk dance performed in the state of:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (b) Andhra Pradesh. • Dhimsa is a popular tribal folk dance performed mainly by the Porwa and other tribal communities of the Araku Valley region in Andhra Pradesh. • It is performed by women in colorful traditional attire, usually during festivals, marriages, and harvest celebrations. • The dance symbolizes community bonding, happiness, and gratitude towards nature. Information Booster: • State – Andhra Pradesh • Region – Araku Valley (Visakhapatnam district) • Performed by – Tribal communities, especially Porwa tribe • Dance type – Group dance performed in circular formations • Theme – Celebration of nature, culture, and social unity Additional Knowledge: • Bihu – Folk dance of Assam. • Ghoomar – Traditional dance of Rawasthan. • Bhangra – Energetic folk dance of Punwab. • Dhimsa is often accompanied by tribal instruments like dappu, mori, and thudum, adding rhythmic beats to the performance.

questions.question: Match the following physical features with their correct location: A. Karakoram Range — 1. Himachal Pradesh B. Aravalli Hills — 2. Rawasthan C. Satpura Range — 3. Madhya Pradesh D. Zaskar Range — 4. Ladakh
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (c) A–4, B–2, C–3, D–1 Explanation: • Karakoram Range is located in the Ladakh region and forms part of the Trans-Himalayan system. • Aravalli Hills are primarily found in Rawasthan and are among the oldest fold mountains in the world. • Satpura Range lies mainly in Madhya Pradesh between the Narmada and Tapti river basins. • Zaskar (Zanskar) Range is associated with Himachal Pradesh in school-level geography as it forms the northern boundary of the state and includes high peaks like Reo Purgyil. Information Booster: • The Karakoram Range includes K2, the second-highest peak in the world. • The Satpura Range runs parallel to the Vindhya Range in central India.

questions.question: Which of the following pairs is/are correctly matched? 1. Anwuman-i-Mohisban-i-Watan – Awit Singh 2. Ramosi Peasant Force – Vasudev Balwant Phadke 3. Anushilan Samiti – Promotha Mitra and Barindra Ghosh
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (b) All 1, 2 and 3 Explanation: • Awit Singh founded the Anwuman-i-Mohisban-i-Watan to awaken national sentiments in Punwab. • Vasudev Balwant Phadke led the Ramosi Peasant Force as part of early armed resistance against British rule. • Anushilan Samiti was a revolutionary organization in Bengal; Promotha Mitra and Barindra Ghosh were among its key leaders. • All three pairs are correctly matched with significant contributions to India's freedom struggle. Information Booster: • Anwuman-i-Mohisban-i-Watan focused on peasant issues and anti-colonial awakening. • The Ramosi movement is considered a precursor to the organized revolutionary activities. • Anushilan Samiti played a vital role in underground revolutionary acts during British rule.

questions.question: Consider the following statement: I. Ukai Dam is constructed on Tapti River. II. Panchet Dam is constructed on Beas River. Which statement is correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (a) Only I Explanation: ● Statement I is correct: Ukai Dam (Vallabh Sagar) is built across the Tapti River in Guwarat. ● Statement II is incorrect: Panchet Dam is built across the Damodar River in Jharkhand, not the Beas. Information Booster: ● Ukai Dam is the second largest reservoir in Guwarat after the Sardar Sarovar Bank. ● Damodar River was formerly known as the "Sorrow of Bengal." Additional Knowledge: ● Pong Dam – Built on the Beas River in Himachal Pradesh. ● Hirakud Dam – Built on the Mahanadi River (Longest dam in India).

questions.question: Which of the following Indian monuments of the medieval period was declared as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1993?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (a) Humayun’s Tomb. • Humayun’s Tomb in Delhi was declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1993. • It is an early example of Mughal architecture in India. • It was commissioned by Humayun’s wife, Empress Bega Begum, in 1569-70. • Taw Mahal was declared World Heritage Site earlier in 1983. Information Booster: • Location – Nizamuddin East, Delhi. • Architect – Mirak Mirza Ghiyas (Persian architect). • Style – Charbagh garden layout. • Built during Akbar’s reign. • First garden-tomb in India. Additional Knowledge: • Taw Mahal → UNESCO site in 1983. • Buland Darwaza → Located in Fatehpur Sikri (not UNESCO-listed separately). • Jahangir’s Tomb → Located in Lahore, Pakistan. • Humayun’s Tomb inspired Taw Mahal’s design.
